title: Graveyard Shift Fraud During Cardholder's Documented Sleep Hours
narrative: Fraudster exploits stolen card credentials between 2:00-5:00 AM in cardholder's timezone while legitimate owner maintains zero historical activity during these hours. Account shows 18 months of daytime-only transactions (txn_hour 7-23), then suddenly processes 4 CNP purchases totaling $1,847 between 02:14-04:33 AM. Transactions originate from IP geolocation 840+ miles from addr1, with dist1 values exceeding 850km. Card1_txn_count_24h spikes from baseline 0.2 to 4 overnight. Fraudster targets digital goods (ProductCD='W') and gaming subscriptions requiring no shipping validation. P_emaildomain switches from consistent gmail.com to temporary burner domain. The cardholder's mobile device shows no app activity or network requests during fraud window, confirmed via device fingerprint timestamps.
indicators: Zero historical transactions during 2:00-5:00 AM hours across 18+ month baseline period
4 transactions totaling $1,847 processed between 02:14-04:33 AM in single session
dist1 values exceed 850km from established addr1 location
Card1_txn_count_24h increases from 0.2 baseline to 4 in 2.5-hour window
P_emaildomain changes from consistent domain to newly-registered burner service
All fraud-window transactions target ProductCD='W' digital goods with instant delivery
Device fingerprint shows zero activity while transactions process
summary: Account with 18 months of daytime-only activity suddenly processes four transactions totaling $1,847 between 2:14-4:33 AM from a location 850+ kilometers away, using a new email domain and targeting instant-delivery digital goods while the cardholder's device shows no activity.
reasoning: A fraudster using stolen credentials deliberately operates during the cardholder's sleep hours to maximize time before discovery, selecting digital products that can be monetized immediately and using a burner email to avoid detection while the legitimate owner is unconscious and unable to intervene.
indicator_explanations: Cardholder has never made a purchase during these overnight hours before
Four purchases worth nearly two thousand dollars hit during dead-of-night hours
Transactions originate from a location over 500 miles from cardholder's home
Card activity spikes from near-zero to four transactions in middle of night
Email suddenly switches from longstanding address to freshly-created throwaway account
All purchases are digital products that deliver instantly with no reversal window
No device interaction detected while transactions are actively being processed
